author: s1na
content:
To summarise the conversation around the minimum history retention required for network stability, a few options came up during the call. They range from 2 weeks to 1 year and each come with a rationale. We also discussed the fact that finalization should play a role into this. If the network doesn't finalize for a while these bounds should move.
- Various limits proposed around the **2 week mark**: weak subjectivity checkpoint (~3500 epochs), blobs retention period (18 days)
- **1 month** came up with the idea to have weak subjectivity checkpoint plus extra buffer (e.g. 8192 epochs for a nice round number)
- **~4-5 months** which coincicdes with the **CL block retention policy**. They've had this for years. Every CL is required to retain 33,024 epochs, regardless of finalization status.
- **1 year** which was stated first in EIP-4444

author: s1na
content: Please let me know if I missed any option that was brought up in the chat
